title: Option Type

statement.markdown:
## Option Type
Option is a monadic type that can be either undefined or a value. It's used to provide a way to handle the presence or absence of a value in a functional way.

### Requirements
1. Define a generic Option type that can hold a value of any type T or be undefined.
2. Implement map and flatMap methods to handle the value inside the Option.
3. Use the Option type to represent a possible user input that either exists or doesn't exist in a web application.

constraints: Must use TypeScript to implement the Option type and its methods
Must handle cases where the value exists and doesn't exist
Must demonstrate understanding of monads in functional programming
